senses.definition: a duration of time

examples.x: The Renaissance period, known for its flourishing of arts and culture, spanned roughly from the 14th to the 17th century.
After a period of intense study and preparation, she finally felt ready to take the entrance exam for medical school.

senses.definition: the end of a specific period of time, particularly one that is expected to last
a span of time

examples.x: She waited patiently for a while before the bus finally arrived.
He studied for a while before taking a short break to rest his eyes.
The children played in the park for a while, enjoying the warm afternoon sun.
After searching for a while, she finally found her misplaced keys under the sofa.
They chatted for a while, catching up on each other's lives before saying goodbye.

senses.definition: a period of time during the day when the sun shines and it is not dark yet
the time when the sun is down and it is dark outside
a period of time during the day in which there is light
at or around 12 o’clock in the middle of the day

examples.x: The sun is usually brightest at midday.
The meeting is scheduled for midday tomorrow.
Midday is the perfect time for a short break.
He left the office just before midday.
She always feels sleepy around midday.

senses.definition: at moments that are not constant or regular

examples.x: She can be a bit reserved at times.
They go hiking in the mountains at times.
The team practices late at times.
At times, she prefers solitude.
He can be unpredictable, getting into heated debates at times.

senses.definition: happening without a pause or break

examples.x: The machine operates in continuous motion, producing items around the clock.
She enjoyed the continuous hum of the city as she walked through the busy streets.
The company's continuous growth over the past decade has made it a market leader.
The continuous rainfall caused flooding in several low-lying areas of the city.
His continuous effort to improve was evident in his work.

senses.definition: without any pause or interruption

examples.x: The conveyor belt moved continuously, transporting goods from one end of the factory to the other.
The music played continuously throughout the event.
The rain fell continuously for hours, causing flooding.
The software runs continuously, ensuring uninterrupted performance.
The traffic flowed continuously on the busy highway.

senses.definition: before the scheduled or expected time

examples.x: She finished the project ahead of time.
We arrived at the venue ahead of time to secure good seats.
Please complete the tasks ahead of time to avoid any delays.
The event preparations were done ahead of time.
He always plans his trips ahead of time.

senses.definition: doing or happening after the time that is usual or expected

examples.x: The late delivery of the package inconvenienced the recipient.
The project submission was late, but the teacher accepted it.
He apologized for being late to the party.
The late response from the client delayed the project timeline.
Due to the late start, they had to rush to finish their work before the deadline.
We shared a late dinner at my house.
I'm late for the meeting.
The train is late by 20 minutes.

senses.definition: to maintain presence over a period

examples.x: The concert lasts for two hours, showcasing the band's greatest hits.
The meeting lasted for over two hours, much longer than we anticipated.
The concert lasted until midnight, filling the night with music.
Right now, the event is lasting longer than expected due to unforeseen delays.
Her excitement lasted only a few moments before she realized the reality of the situation.

senses.definition: to need a specific amount of time to do something or for something to be done or happen

examples.x: Learning a new language can take several months of consistent practice.
Completing the project will likely take a few weeks of dedicated effort.
Cooking a homemade meal from scratch can take more time than ordering takeout.
The repair work on the car is estimated to take at least a couple of days.
Mastering a musical instrument can take years of practice and dedication.

senses.definition: happening or arriving at the time expected or arranged

examples.x: She is always punctual for her appointments.
The train's punctual arrival impressed the passengers.
Being punctual shows respect for others' time.
His punctual habits earned him a good reputation at work.
They expect their employees to be punctual every morning.

senses.definition: during something's entire period of time

examples.x: The singer maintained perfect pitch throughout.
Prices remained stable throughout.
Her optimism never wavered throughout.
The rain was heavy throughout.

senses.definition: taking place unexpectedly or done quickly

examples.x: The sudden noise startled everyone in the room.
They made a sudden decision to leave for the beach early in the morning.
A sudden rainstorm forced them to seek shelter under a tree.
His sudden departure from the company left his colleagues surprised.
The car came to a sudden stop to avoid hitting the deer on the road.

senses.definition: at predictable, equal time periods

examples.x: The committee meets regularly, on the first Monday of every month.
She exercises regularly, three times a week without fail.
The magazine is published regularly, every two weeks.
He checks his email regularly throughout the day.
The bus runs regularly, arriving every 15 minutes.

senses.definition: happening or done frequently
taking place or existing now

examples.x: The immediate concern for the company was how to handle the financial crisis.
Her immediate focus is on completing the project by the deadline.
The doctor's immediate priority was stabilizing the patient.
The government's immediate response to the disaster was to send relief teams.
His immediate challenge was finding a place to stay after moving to the new city.

senses.definition: after or at the end of a series of events or an extended period

examples.x: He eventually became the manager of the company.
Despite initial setbacks, they eventually succeeded in completing the challenging project.
They faced many obstacles, but eventually, they overcame all difficulties and reached their goal.
She struggled to learn the language at first, but eventually, she became fluent through consistent practice.
After years of hard work, he eventually achieved his dream of starting his own business.

senses.definition: in the time following a specific action, moment, or event

examples.x: She completed her work, and afterward, she went for a walk.
He finished his meeting, and afterward, he took a break to grab some coffee.
Afterward, the storm subsided, leaving behind a tranquil evening.
The family enjoyed a delicious meal, and everyone shared stories afterward.
She didn't plan to attend the workshop, but afterward, she realized how valuable it was.

senses.definition: used to refer to a time in the past, showing how much time has passed before the present moment

examples.x: She moved to this city three years ago.
They met a long time ago in college.
I finished reading that book a month ago.
The company was founded over a century ago.
He left the office just a few minutes ago.

senses.definition: continuously, persistently, or without pause

examples.x: The air conditioner runs all the time, it never turns off.
She worries all the time, even in her sleep.
This street is noisy all the time, day and night.
"His leg hurts all the time, with no relief.
The server crashes all the time because it's overloaded.

senses.definition: at a considerable distance in time

examples.x: The technology we have now will seem primitive far in the future.
The ruins were built far in the past, long before modern civilization.
If you look far back in history, you'll find fascinating changes in human society.
Planning far ahead can help avoid unforeseen problems.

senses.definition: after every 60 minutes

examples.x: He checks his email hourly for updates.
The train schedule is available hourly.
The weather forecast is updated hourly.
The data backups are performed hourly for safety.
The bus departs hourly from the station.

senses.definition: with no delay and at once

examples.x: The digital transaction was completed instantly.
Pressing the button unlocked the door instantly.
Upon hearing the news, she reacted instantly.
The software provided results instantly after the analysis.
The online message was delivered instantly to the recipient.

senses.definition: occurring or existing right at this moment

examples.x: The present situation requires immediate attention and action.
The present company enjoyed a relaxing afternoon at the park.
The present circumstances call for adaptability and resilience.
The present generation faces unique challenges compared to previous ones.

senses.definition: done or existed before the present time

examples.x: Her past experiences shaped her perspective on life.
Past mistakes served as valuable lessons for future decision-making.
The past events of the company shaped its current policies and strategies.
His past achievements continue to inspire those around him.

senses.definition: coming in to existence or happening after this moment

examples.x: Planning for future projects is essential for the company's growth.
Future advancements in technology will revolutionize various industries.
She eagerly anticipates future opportunities for personal and professional development.
The future generations will inherit the consequences of our actions today.
Future innovations in medicine hold the promise of curing currently incurable diseases.

senses.definition: before the present or specified time

examples.x: She had already left when I arrived.
By the time the show started, we had already found our seats.
I already knew the answer before he explained it.
She has already packed her bags for the trip.
He has already read that book twice.

senses.definition: at the present time

examples.x: The company is currently working on a new product launch.
I am currently responding to your inquiry.
She is currently studying for her upcoming exams.
Currently, the stock market is experiencing a period of volatility.
The restaurant is currently closed for renovations.

senses.definition: at any point in time

examples.x: If ever you need help, just call me.
Nothing ever scared her, not even thunderstorms.
Don't ever speak to me like that again.
Nothing ever disturbed his calm demeanor.
Did she ever mention her plans to you?

senses.definition: used to describe a period of time that has no end

examples.x: She promised to love him forever.
The memory of their wedding day will stay with them forever.
The book will remain a classic forever.
He will be remembered forever for his contributions to science.
Their bond felt forever, beyond the passage of time.

senses.definition: very recently or only before this moment

examples.x: She just finished her homework and was about to relax.
I was about to leave when you called me just a moment ago.
He had just stepped out of the room when the phone rang.
The package was delivered just before you arrived home.
We were just talking about you when you walked in.

senses.definition: at the same time but often somewhere else

examples.x: She started cooking dinner; meanwhile, her husband set the table.
The kids played in the backyard, meanwhile the adults prepared the food for the barbecue.
She was at the grocery store, and meanwhile, I was waiting at home for her call.

senses.definition: before the present moment or a specific time

examples.x: She had previously worked for a different company before joining the current one.
I had previously visited the city for a conference before moving here for work.
The current system operates more efficiently than the one used previously.
I had previously attended a similar workshop, so I was familiar with the content.
The project had been proposed and discussed previously by the team, but no concrete plans were made.

senses.definition: at or toward a distance in time

examples.x: Her birthday is just three days away.
The tournament is still weeks away.
Summer break is only a month away now.
The deadline is two hours away.
Graduation is a whole year away.

senses.definition: close in time to a moment

examples.x: The near future holds many exciting possibilities.
The deadline for the project is near, so we need to finalize everything soon.

senses.definition: not conforming to established rules, patterns, or norms

examples.x: The irregular intervals between the train departures made it difficult for passengers to plan their trips.
The irregular timing of the meetings often caused confusion among the team members.
She arrived at irregular intervals to class, making it hard to catch up with the lessons.
Her irregular work schedule made it challenging to maintain a consistent routine.
The irregular behavior of the customer raised concerns among the store staff.
Her irregular speech pattern puzzled her colleagues, who found it difficult to understand her.
